D6 Display L6 Create Reference

Dynamic Metadata (Dolby Vision)

a single fixed tone curve cannot flatter a dark interior and a blazing sunset at once — per-shot metadata refits the curve scene by scene

HDR10 ships one static tone curve sized for the brightest moment of the whole film. On a display that cannot reach the mastering peak, that single curve reserves headroom for highlights a dark scene never uses — so the dark scene comes out dim. Dolby Vision carries per-scene metadata (min/max/average light) and refits the curve for each shot.

Both rows show the same two scenes tone-mapped to one modest display. Flip from static HDR10 to dynamic Dolby Vision: the sunset keeps its highlight roll-off, but the dark interior is finally allowed to fill the display instead of staying buried.
